schema.description: Benefits schema covering the full lifecycle of employee benefits — from catalog discovery to inquiry submission, HR assignment, and resolution tracking. Built across two tables: a read-mostly catalog of available benefit plans, and a transactional inquiry table tracking employee questions and requests.

benefit_category.description: Standardized benefit category list shared across both BenefitsCatalog and BenefitsInquiry. Used to group plans and filter inquiries by type.

values.health: Health and medical insurance plans including hospital, prescription, and specialist coverage.
values.dental: Dental care plans covering preventive, basic, and major services.
values.vision: Vision care plans covering eye exams, glasses, and contact lenses.
values.retirement: Retirement savings plans such as 401k, pension, or RRSP.
values.life_insurance: Life insurance coverage provided to employees or available for purchase.
values.disability: Short-term and long-term disability income protection plans.
values.wellness: Wellness programs including gym subsidies, mental health support, and health screenings.
values.time_off: Leave policies including vacation, sick leave, parental leave, and sabbaticals.
values.employee_assistance: Employee Assistance Programs (EAP) offering counselling and support services.
values.professional_development: Learning, training, tuition reimbursement, and certification support.
values.commuter: Commuter benefits including transit passes, parking subsidies, and bike programs.
values.other: Any benefit that does not fall into the above categories.

BenefitsCatalog.description: Reference table listing every benefit plan the company offers. Read-mostly — updated by HR during open enrollment or when plans change. Queried by employees and the AI agent to discover available benefits.

id.description: Unique system identifier for this benefit plan record.

benefit_name.description: Human-readable name of the benefit plan as it appears in communications and the employee portal.

benefit_description.description: Full description of what the benefit covers, exclusions, and key features. Primary text the AI agent uses to answer employee questions.

category.description: Category this benefit plan belongs to. Links to the same category list used in BenefitsInquiry for filtering.

eligibility.description: Who qualifies for this benefit and under what conditions (e.g. employment type, tenure, location). Null means universally available.

cost.description: Monthly cost charged to the employee. Zero means fully employer-funded.
cost.rule: employee_net_cost = cost - employer_contribution is never applicable; cost and employer_contribution are independent values

employer_contribution.description: Monthly amount the employer contributes toward this benefit. The total plan value = cost + employer_contribution.

total_plan_value.description: Derived total monthly value of this benefit plan.

enrollment_period.description: When employees can enroll or make changes. Typically open enrollment window plus new hire grace period.

contact_info.description: HR or provider contact details for employees who need direct assistance with this benefit.

website_url.description: URL of the provider portal or plan details page where employees can view full coverage information.

provider.description: Name of the insurance company or vendor administering this benefit (e.g. Blue Cross, Delta Dental).

keywords.description: Space-separated search terms that help employees and the AI agent find this benefit. Supplements benefit_description for natural language queries.
keywords.example: dental crown filling orthodontics braces cleaning

active.description: Whether this benefit is currently available for enrollment. Inactive plans remain in the catalog for historical reference but are excluded from search results.

BenefitsInquiry.description: Transactional table that records every employee benefits interaction — searches, enrollment requests, information questions, change requests, and complaints. One record per employee interaction. Managed by HR and partially automated.

id.description: Unique system identifier for this inquiry.

employee.description: The employee who submitted this inquiry. References the ServiceNow sys_user table.

values.search: Employee searched for benefit information — no action required, informational only.
values.enrollment: Employee wants to enroll in a benefit plan. Requires HR action to process.
values.information: Employee is asking a question about a benefit. Requires HR to provide an answer.
values.change: Employee wants to modify an existing benefit election (e.g. add a dependent).
values.complaint: Employee has an issue or grievance about a benefit. Elevated priority, requires prompt response.

inquiry_type.description: The nature of the employee interaction. Drives automatic priority assignment via automation.

benefit_category.description: The benefit category this inquiry relates to. Soft link to BenefitsCatalog — not a hard FK to a specific plan.

search_terms.description: The exact terms the employee typed or spoke when searching. Populated for inquiry_type='search'. Used for analytics.

description.description: Full text of the employee's question, request, or complaint. Primary field HR reads to understand what is needed.

values.open: Inquiry has been submitted but no HR action has been taken yet.
values.in_progress: An HR representative is actively working on this inquiry.
values.resolved: HR has provided an answer or completed the requested action. Awaiting employee confirmation.
values.closed: Inquiry is fully complete. No further action required.

status.description: Lifecycle state of the inquiry. Progresses from open through to closed.

values.low: Informational or non-urgent inquiry with no time sensitivity.
values.medium: Standard inquiry requiring a response within normal SLA.
values.high: Urgent inquiry — typically enrollment deadlines or complaints — requiring prompt attention.
values.urgent: Critical issue requiring immediate HR response.

priority.description: Urgency level of the inquiry. Automatically set by automation based on inquiry_type. complaint → high, enrollment → medium. Can be overridden by HR.

assigned_to.description: The HR representative handling this inquiry. Automatically populated by automation when the record is created. Null until automation runs.

resolution_notes.description: HR's response, answer, or action taken. Must be populated before status can be set to 'resolved'.

inquiry_date.description: Timestamp when the inquiry was created. Automatically set to current date/time on record creation. Never manually edited.

satisfaction_rating.description: Employee's satisfaction score after resolution (1 = very dissatisfied, 5 = very satisfied). Only meaningful when status is 'resolved' or 'closed'. Never auto-set.

relationships.description: Many inquiries can belong to one employee.
Many inquiries can be assigned to one HR user.

benefit_category on an inquiry matches the same enum used in the catalog but does NOT reference a specific catalog record. An inquiry about 'dental' applies to all active dental plans, not one specific row.
An employee browsing BenefitsCatalog may submit a BenefitsInquiry referencing the category they found. No hard FK — the link is through benefit_category.

automationRules.trigger: on create or update of BenefitsInquiry
automationRules.description: Finds an active HR user (role contains 'hr') and sets assigned_to automatically. If no HR user is found, assigned_to remains null.

automationRules.trigger: on create or update of BenefitsInquiry
automationRules.description: Sets priority based on inquiry_type. complaint → high, enrollment → medium. Other types are not overridden by this rule.
Logs a notification message when status transitions. Does not send email — informational log only.

automationRules.trigger: when BenefitsInquiry with inquiry_type='search' is created
automationRules.description: Logs search_terms and benefit_category for analytics. Does not write back to the record.

businessRules.description: BenefitsInquiry.status cannot be set to 'resolved' unless resolution_notes is populated.
satisfaction_rating is only meaningful when status is 'resolved' or 'closed'. A rating on an open inquiry should be ignored.
businessRules.expression: satisfaction_rating is valid ONLY WHEN status IN ('resolved', 'closed')

businessRules.description: priority is automatically set by automationRules. HR can override it but it should not be set by the employee.
Only BenefitsCatalog records where active=true should be returned in employee-facing searches. Inactive records exist for historical reference only.
businessRules.expression: employee queries MUST filter BenefitsCatalog WHERE active == true

businessRules.description: total_plan_value is always derived and never set directly.
inquiry_date is set automatically at creation and must never be manually changed.
businessRules.expression: inquiry_date is write-once, set at record creation

x_roles.description: Role definitions governing who can read or write benefits data.

hr_benefits_admin.description: HR administrators who manage the benefits catalog and handle employee inquiries.

employee.description: Employees who can browse the catalog and submit inquiries.
